title: OpenAI releases LifeSciBench to test AI on realistic life-science research work
friendlyTitle: OpenAI made a harder test for life-science AI
summary: OpenAI introduced LifeSciBench, an expert-authored benchmark with 750 tasks across applied life-science workflows such as experiment design, evidence handling, analysis, validation, translation, and scientific communication. The benchmark uses detailed rubrics from scientists rather than simple right-or-wrong trivia questions.
friendlySummary: LifeSciBench tests AI on real research-style tasks, not just simple biology questions. Experts wrote and reviewed the tasks and grading rules.
whyItMatters: Scientific AI tools need to be evaluated on messy, expert work where caveats and decisions matter. Better benchmarks can help labs and biotech teams judge whether a model is a useful collaborator or merely fluent on biology facts.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help scientists choose AI tools that are actually useful for research decisions, not just good at sounding smart.
